title: Soldier Caffeine Energy Simulation
description: A soldier stands in a training field with a giant coffee mug acting as an energy reservoir. The user pours coffee doses into the soldier to control stamina, speed, and alertness stats governed by conservation of total energy. Interactions include pouring, draining, redistributing stats, and full undo/redo of every action.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with ES modules from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing a soldier energy simulation. Load coffeeMug.glb scaled 2x on the left and soldier.glb on the right on a flat ground plane. Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,8) looking at origin, OrbitControls, ambient light (0.5) and a directional light from (5,10,7). Add HDR-style background color #1a1a2e.

SIMULATION RULES: The coffee mug holds 100 energy units initially. The soldier has three stats: Stamina, Speed, Alertness, each starting at 0. Total energy across mug + all soldier stats is ALWAYS conserved at 100 (conservation law). Display all values in a HUD overlay.

INTERACTIONS (10-14 steps possible): (1) POUR button transfers 10 energy from mug to a selected stat. (2) Three radio buttons select which stat receives energy. (3) DRAIN button returns 10 energy from selected stat back to mug. (4) REDISTRIBUTE slider appears when clicking soldier — lets user split one stat's energy across the other two stats proportionally (multi-input coupling: two range sliders that are linked so their sum equals the source stat value). (5) BOOST ALL button evenly distributes all mug energy across three stats. (6) RESET SOLDIER returns all soldier energy to mug. (7) Full UNDO and REDO buttons that track every state change in a history stack.

VISUAL FEEDBACK: Animate the coffee mug's Y position bobbing when energy leaves it. Tint the soldier mesh color based on dominant stat — green for stamina, blue for speed, yellow for alertness — using lerped material color. Show a particle stream (simple sphere geometries) flowing from mug to soldier on pour and reverse on drain. Scale the soldier slightly (1.0 to 1.3) proportional to total soldier energy. The mug should have a visible fill-level indicator — a brown cylinder inside the mug whose scaleY maps to remaining mug energy percentage.

UI: Use an HTML overlay panel with styled buttons, radio inputs, sliders, and undo/redo controls. Display current mug energy and all three stats numerically. Show history step count. All interactions must enforce conservation — disable pour when mug is empty, disable drain when stat is 0.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  mugEnergy: number,         // energy units remaining in coffee mug (0-100)
  soldierStamina: number,         // stamina stat value (0-100)
  soldierSpeed: number,         // speed stat value (0-100)
  soldierAlertness: number,         // alertness stat value (0-100)
  selectedStat: string,         // which stat is currently selected (stamina|speed|alertness)
  redistributeMode: boolean,         // whether redistribute UI is active
  redistributeSourceStat: string,         // stat being redistributed from
  redistributeTarget1Amount: number,         // amount going to first target stat during redistribute
  redistributeTarget2Amount: number,         // amount going to second target stat during redistribute
  totalEnergyConserved: number,         // sum of all energy values, must always equal 100
  interactionCount: number,         // total number of user interactions performed
}
